articles.title: Motorcyclist killed in Mississauga crash
articles.summary: A motorcyclist was killed Sunday in a collision in Mississauga’s Meadowvale neighbourhood. Peel Regional Police say the crash happened near Argentia and Kitimat Roads some time around 9:45 p.m. The motorcyclist was pronounced dead at the scene.  Authorities say the driver of the vehicle remained at the scene. No other details were immediately available.

articles.title: CN Tower honours victims of Salsa on St. Clair shooting
articles.summary: The CN Tower is dimming its lights in honour of the victims of the Salsa on St. Clair shooting that happened Saturday night. Officials say the lights will dim at the top of every hour Sunday evening in homage of the victims. Two men were killed at the festival during an exchange of gunfire that […]

articles.title: Marina Mabrey scores 30 as Tempo hold off Liberty in Montreal, ending 4-game skid
articles.summary: The Toronto Tempo held off a late-game charge from the New York Liberty on Sunday, winning 93-91 to end a four-game losing streak in their second of two games at the Bell Centre in Montreal.

articles.title: Bonnie Crombie announces intention to run for mayor of Mississauga
articles.summary: After a short stint leading the Ontario Liberals, Bonnie Crombie is looking to get her old job back as mayor of Mississauga, Ont.

articles.title: Bonnie Crombie confirms she’s running to be Mississauga mayor again
articles.summary: Bonnie Crombie wants to be mayor of Mississauga, once again. Crombie confirmed Sunday in an interview with 680NewsRadio that she intends to register as a candidate on Tuesday in the upcoming municipal election. She shared a messaged with some of her supporters over the weekend advising them of her decision. “Please join me as I […]

articles.title: 64-year-old woman dead after being struck by dump truck in Ajax: police
articles.summary: Durham police say a 64-year-old woman is dead after being struck by a dump truck in Ajax on Saturday.

articles.title: Man, 68, dead after being struck by vehicle in Pickering parking lot: Durham police
articles.summary: Durham police say a 68-year-old man is dead after being struck by a vehicle in a Pickering parking lot Saturday.

articles.title: E-bike battery fire sends 3 to hospital in Scarborough
articles.summary: Fire officials are once again warning about the dangers of lithium-ion batteries following an early morning fire at a Scarborough high-rise on Sunday. Fire crews responded to a two-alarm blaze on Lawrence Avenue East and Morningside Avenue at 7:30 a.m., where they were met with heavy smoke on the second floor of the building. Paramedics […]

articles.title: Woman, 64, struck and killed by dump truck in Ajax
articles.summary: Police in Durham Region are investigating after a 64-year-old woman was struck and killed by a dump truck in Ajax. Investigators say just before 4 p.m. Saturday, the truck was making a right-hand turn from Harwood Avenue South onto Hunt Street when it struck a woman. Police say life-saving measures were performed but she was […]

articles.title: Man, 69, dead after being struck by vehicle in Pickering parking lot
articles.summary: A 69-year-old man is dead after being struck by a vehicle in Pickering. Police in Durham Region say a black SUV was driving through the parking lot of the Brookdale Centre near Highway 2 and Walnut Lane just after 10 p.m. Saturday when it struck a pedestrian, trapping the man under the vehicle. “Responding police […]

articles.title: Woman dead after being hit by truck in Ajax: Durham police
articles.summary: A woman has died after she was struck by a commercial truck while walking in Ajax Saturday afternoon, according to Durham Regional Police Service.

articles.title: 1 dead, 2 others in hospital after late-night shooting in North York
articles.summary: A 22-year-old man is dead after a shooting in North York late Friday night, Toronto police say.

articles.title: Parking rules next to Toronto park didn't consider accessible permit holders: park volunteer
articles.summary: A slight change to parking rules on a small stub of street adjacent to Dufferin Grove Park has led to a large controversy over accessible parking permit holders.
